welcome.title: GitUniverse
welcome.subtitle: Interactive Solar System Simulation
welcome.wipMessage: This passion project is continuously evolving. Follow me for updates, or reach out if you want to beta test features or share ideas!
welcome.blurb: An interactive solar system you can explore, perturb, and play with. Click around -- everything is meant to be touched.
welcome.surveyPrompt: Got 2 minutes? Tell us what you'd like to see next
changes: Speed of Light lesson - watch light travel across the solar system
Rayleigh scattering - realistic sky colors and sunsets based on atmosphere composition
Bug fixes and translation loading improvements

feedback.sourceDescription: Where did you hear about GitUniverse? (optional)
feedback.sourceLabel: Where did you hear about GitUniverse?
feedback.featuresDescription: What features would you like to see in GitUniverse?
feedback.thankYou: Thank you! I appreciate your feedback.

betaTester.title: Want to be a beta tester?
betaTester.description: Hey - I don't actually know you, but locally I can see you've opened GitUniverse over {{threshold}} times, so I figured you might enjoy it. Would you like early access to new builds before they go public?
betaTester.privacy: This count is stored only in your browser. Nothing is sent unless you submit your email below.
betaTester.thankYou: Thank you! I'll be in touch with the next beta build.
betaTester.inlineDesc: You've opened GitUniverse a lot. Want early access to upcoming builds? Drop your email below.

starEditor.scaleTip: Tip: enable True-to-Scale in Settings to see the star's actual expanded size. Normal mode caps star size so it doesn't overlap planet orbits, so an evolved star can look smaller than reality.

help.analyticsDescription: We use Umami, a privacy-focused analytics tool. Umami:
help.dataNote: We only collect anonymous, aggregated data (page views, browser type, country).

skyView.movementHint: Drag to look around -- WASD to walk -- Shift to run

stars.sun: Our home star - a G-type main sequence yellow dwarf
stars.proximaCentauri: Nearest star to the Sun - a dim red dwarf 4.2 light-years away
stars.siriusA: Brightest star in Earth's night sky - a hot white A-type star
stars.arcturus: Brightest star in the northern sky - an aging orange giant
stars.aldebaran: The eye of Taurus - a cool orange giant 65 light-years away
stars.pollux: Closest giant star to Earth - an orange giant with a confirmed exoplanet
stars.rigel: Blue supergiant in Orion - one of the most luminous stars visible to the naked eye
stars.betelgeuse: Red supergiant in Orion's shoulder - could explode as a supernova any time
stars.antares: Heart of Scorpius - a massive red supergiant nearing the end of its life
stars.deneb: Tail of Cygnus the Swan - an extremely luminous blue-white supergiant
stars.vyCanisMajoris: One of the largest known stars - a red hypergiant 1,420 times the Sun's radius
stars.uyScuti: Among the largest stars ever measured - a red supergiant 1,708 times the Sun's radius

skyViewTips.eclipticStep1: Notice how all the planets line up across the sky? That's not a coincidence. They all orbit the Sun in nearly the same flat plane, called the ecliptic.
skyViewTips.eclipticStep2: Ancient astronomers noticed this band thousands of years ago. From any planet in the solar system, you'd see the other planets trace a similar path across your sky, since they all share nearly the same orbital plane.

ecliptic.title: The Ecliptic
ecliptic.desc: All planets orbit the Sun in nearly the same flat plane. From any surface in the solar system, they appear as a line across the sky.
ecliptic.action: Select any planet, then tap Sky View to see it yourself.

habitableZones.title: Habitable Zones
habitableZones.desc: The region around a star where liquid water could exist on a planet's surface. Edit a star's properties and watch the zone shift.
habitableZones.action: Select the Sun, then edit its mass or luminosity.

gravity.title: Gravitational Interactions
gravity.desc: Every object pulls on every other object. Spawn a massive body near a planet and watch real physics unfold.
gravity.action: Open Spawn, create a Jupiter-mass planet near Earth.

videos.title: Videos
videos.channelDesc: Tutorials, demos and behind-the-scenes for GitUniverse.

scatteringPlayground.title: Scattering Playground
scatteringPlayground.desc: Mix atmospheric gases and see how sky color changes in real time. Explore the physics of Rayleigh and Mie scattering.

support.title: Support GitUniverse
support.description: GitUniverse is a free educational tool. You can support the project by sharing it, or following along on YouTube, Substack, and Bluesky where I post development updates.

crash.title: Something went wrong
crash.description: GitUniverse ran into an unexpected error. You can help us fix it by sending a report.
crash.descriptionPlaceholder: I was looking at Saturn when...
crash.thankYou: Thank you! Your report will help us fix this.
crash.emailPlaceholder: So we can follow up with you
crash.bugReportDescription: Found something broken? Please be as detailed as possible -- what you were doing, what you expected, and what happened instead.
crash.bugReportPlaceholder: I was viewing Saturn's rings when the app froze...

quickTour.askPrompt: Want some tips to get started?

scale.title: Not to Scale
scale.body: Planets are enlarged so you can see them. Toggle this to see true sizes.

time.title: Time
time.body: Speed up, slow down, or pause.
